html:
Johns Manville Product Lines and Insulation Integration

Johns Manville is a manufacturer whose commercial roofing membranes and, notably, roof insulation products come up regularly on Spartanburg County projects, since JM is a significant supplier in the insulation board market alongside its membrane line. This page covers factual product and spec details relevant to their commercial roofing offering.

Product Categories

Johns Manville produces TPO and EPDM membrane systems along with a broad line of polyisocyanurate and other insulation board products used across many roof assemblies well beyond their own membrane line. That dual position in both membrane and insulation manufacturing means JM insulation sometimes appears as a component in assemblies specified with a different manufacturer's membrane, which is worth understanding when reviewing warranty documentation for a mixed-manufacturer assembly. It also means JM's insulation catalog is frequently updated on its own schedule, separate from any changes to their membrane line, so a spec pulled from an older project file can reference discontinued board options.

Spec Sheet Details

JM's membrane data sheets list the same core performance figures as other manufacturers: thickness, reinforcement, tensile and tear strength, and elongation, referenced against applicable ASTM standards. We check the current data sheet for the exact product specified on a Spartanburg County job, since insulation board thickness and facer type from JM's line also has its own spec sheet that needs to be confirmed separately when their insulation is paired with membrane from any manufacturer.

Insulation Product Considerations

Johns Manville's polyiso insulation boards come in various thicknesses and facer types, and R-value, compressive strength, and moisture resistance figures vary by product within their line. On industrial buildings near BMW's Greer manufacturing operations and other large-footprint facilities we work on, insulation selection affects both energy code compliance and the overall assembly's tested wind uplift rating, so insulation spec gets the same scrutiny as membrane spec rather than being treated as an interchangeable commodity layer. Facer type in particular affects how insulation performs under a fully adhered versus mechanically fastened membrane, and a board designed for one attachment method doesn't always carry the same rated performance under the other, which is a detail that's easy to miss when a spec is copied forward from a prior project without rechecking the current data sheet.

FM and UL Assembly Ratings

Johns Manville maintains FM Approvals and UL listings for tested assembly configurations combining their membrane and insulation products in specific arrangements. Since JM insulation is sometimes used with another manufacturer's membrane, we verify whether the specific combination on a given project has a valid tested assembly rating, since not every membrane-insulation pairing across manufacturers has been jointly tested and rated.

  • Confirm the current JM data sheet for both the membrane and any JM insulation being specified
  • Verify whether a mixed-manufacturer assembly (JM insulation with another brand's membrane) has a valid tested rating
  • Check insulation R-value and facer type against the project's energy code requirements
  • Review JM's material-only versus system warranty terms before finalizing the contract
  • Confirm compressive strength rating on insulation for roofs with regular foot traffic
  • Keep safety data sheets on file for both membrane and insulation products installed

Warranty Structure

Johns Manville offers material-only and system-level warranty tiers on their roofing products, with terms and inspection requirements documented in their published warranty language. Because insulation and membrane can come from different manufacturers on the same roof, we clarify with owners upfront which manufacturer's warranty actually covers which component, since a membrane warranty from one manufacturer doesn't automatically extend to insulation supplied by a different one.

Regional Fit: Compressive Strength and Humidity

Insulation compressive strength matters more than it might seem on Spartanburg County industrial buildings with regular rooftop equipment traffic, since standard roofing insulation can compress or deteriorate under repeated point loads from service technicians and equipment. We check JM's compressive strength ratings against expected traffic patterns, and we account for humid Upstate summer conditions when evaluating any insulation product's long-term moisture resistance claims, since sustained humidity exposure is a more realistic long-term condition here than a brief seasonal event. We also account for how quickly a wet insulation board can lose compressive strength before it's fully replaced, which factors into how we sequence tear-off and dry-in on a multi-day project during Spartanburg's unpredictable summer storm pattern.

Questions About Johns Manville Products

Does Johns Manville only make insulation, or do they make membrane too?

They manufacture both. JM produces TPO and EPDM membrane systems as well as a substantial line of roof insulation products used broadly across the industry.

Can JM insulation be used under any manufacturer's membrane?

It depends on whether that specific combination has a tested and rated assembly. We verify this before specifying a mixed-manufacturer configuration.

Does using JM insulation affect our membrane warranty from a different manufacturer?

It can, depending on that membrane manufacturer's warranty terms regarding approved insulation products. We confirm compatibility requirements before finalizing any mixed-manufacturer spec.

How do we know which insulation thickness we need?

Insulation thickness is driven by energy code requirements and the specific R-value target for the project, which we calculate based on current code and building use.

Is JM insulation more resistant to moisture than other brands?

Moisture resistance varies by specific product and facer type within JM's line, so we reference the current data sheet for the exact product rather than making a blanket comparison across manufacturers. We also check the facer type and any published perm rating for the specific board, since two products with similar labeled thickness can behave quite differently once installed under a fully adhered membrane in Spartanburg's humid climate.
